campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review financial investments from March 2026 and heard a presentation from the regional transit authority. The council also approved a new policy that prevents the city from letting federal agents use city property and buildings, and discussed ways to recruit and keep city employees.
keyTopics: March 2026 investment report review
Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority presentation
New policy restricting federal agency access to city property
City employee recruitment and retention strategies

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public meeting on March 18, 2026 where residents had a chance to speak about a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing public input, the council introduced a new ordinance (local law) and decided to speed up the process by skipping the usual first reading, planning to vote on final approval at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather community feedback
New ordinance introduced and expedited for faster approval
Vote on final adoption scheduled for next council meeting
Council waived standard first reading procedure

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from earlier in the month and review the town's finances for February. The council authorized the Town Manager to sign several contracts for construction work and professional services.
keyTopics: Approved February 2026 financial and investment report
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other vendors
Approved three-year professional services agreement
Reviewed and approved previous meeting minutes

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 5, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve several projects and agreements. They discussed replacing an old elevator at the Terminal, reviewed cleanup services for homeless encampments, and approved a digital advertising deal with Clear Channel. The council also officially backed a firefighter memorial event happening in District 7.
keyTopics: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project - infrastructure upgrade
Encampment Cleanup Services - proposals for managing homeless camps
Clear Channel Digital Signage Agreement - large advertising displays on city property
Light the Night for Fallen Firefighters - memorial event funding approval

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to approve meeting minutes and hear from residents during public comment. The main focus was reviewing and deciding which neighborhood improvement projects and community programs would receive funding for the upcoming 2026-27 fiscal year.
keyTopics: Neighborhood grant funding decisions for 2026-27
Public comment period for residents
Community attendance at neighborhood meetings
Meeting minutes approval
Committee updates and staff reports

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special meeting on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for various city boards and commissions. Residents had the opportunity to provide public comment both in person and online during the session.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
Public comment period (in-person and online options)
Special study session format

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 5, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council met to discuss buying or selling a piece of property on Finch Avenue with the local school district, with the city manager and attorney leading negotiations about the price and payment terms. The council also reviewed the Silicon Valley Hopper program and talked about getting grant money to fund it in the future.
keyTopics: Property deal negotiations with Cupertino Union School District for 10480 Finch Avenue
Working out purchase price and payment terms for the property
Silicon Valley Hopper program overview and future grant funding strategy

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large promotional signs to be temporarily installed on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup events. The council also reviewed and approved a new labor agreement with the city's firefighters for 2026-2029.
keyTopics: Temporary supergraphic signs around Levi's Stadium for FIFA World Cup 2026
New 3-year labor contract with Santa Clara firefighters
One agenda item was withdrawn by staff before the meeting

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to plan the city's spending for the next several years. They discussed the budget for 2026-27, approved a plan for new parks and trails in the Innovation District, and made updates to how the council conducts its meetings and handles procedures.
keyTopics: 5-year capital spending plan (2027-2031)
2026-27 operating budget presentation
Innovation District parks and trails master plan
Updates to city council meeting rules and procedures

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to discuss routine business items and a major housing development proposal. A developer is asking permission to split a 1-acre vacant lot on Old Trace Road into 9 residential lots to build 9 new single-family homes plus 7 smaller accessory dwelling units. The council also elected new leadership positions.
keyTopics: Proposed subdivision of Old Trace Road lot into 9 single-family homes + 7 accessory units
Project designated as environmentally exempt under state housing law
Property zoned for residential estate use
Election of new Council Chair and Vice Chair
Director's report and meeting scheduling updates
